meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Finish Your App Fast (Founder Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your build. Use this SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP faster and start generating revenue today.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' trap is usually a failure of scope, not skill. By adopting a SaaS starter kit, you offload the infrastructure—auth, billing, and database—allowing you to focus exclusively on your core value proposition. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate your features to market-ready status.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'almost-finished' projects. You spend weeks configuring OAuth, wrestling with Stripe API keys, and debugging database migrations while your core idea sits stagnant. The excitement of the initial commit fades into the drudgery of boilerplate, and suddenly, you’re just another founder with a local repo that never saw production.

It doesn't have to be this way. The difference between a hobbyist and a founder who ships is the leverage they employ. By utilizing a high-octane SaaS starter kit, you bypass the infrastructure grind. You aren't building a product from scratch; you are standing on the shoulders of optimized architecture, enabling you to ship your MVP in days, not months.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP Scope
steps.description: Strip away all non-essential features. Focus strictly on the one problem your customer will pay to solve immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a clean SaaS project structure focusing on a single core feature. Include user authentication and stripe subscription integration as the primary focus.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Analyze my core feature and define the leanest possible MVP scope to reach production in 7 days.'

steps.title: Automate Infrastructure Scaffolding
steps.description: Use your starter kit to deploy a production-ready stack (Next.js, Tailwind, Supabase/Prisma) without manual config.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize the project directory with a production-grade SaaS starter kit template. Configure Supabase for auth and database management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Setup the project environment and verify that all environment variables for Supabase and Stripe are correctly mapped.'

steps.title: Implement Revenue-First Features
steps.description: Build the payment flow before the design. If users can't pay, you don't have a business, you have a prototype.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build the Stripe checkout session handler and user subscription status check middleware.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a secure server-side API route for Stripe checkout sessions and a webhook handler for subscription updates.'

steps.title: Rapid Feature Iteration
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write functional components based on your specific business logic, avoiding manual boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a CRUD interface for the core product resource based on my database schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Refactor this component to be fully responsive and integrate with the existing database schema.'

steps.title: Ship to Production
steps.description: Automate your deployment pipeline so that every commit is a potential launch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Vercel deployment settings and environment variable synchronization for production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Audit the codebase for production readiness and provide the final deployment command sequence.'

pro_tips: Never build custom auth; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 40+ hours of security engineering.
Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Dollar' over feature richness; if the checkout works, you have a business.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; technical debt is the primary cause of abandoned projects.
Ignore pixel-perfect design until you have five paying users; functionality wins, aesthetics are secondary.
Adopt a 'Ship-Every-Friday' cadence; forced deadlines with Claude Code prevent scope creep.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, immediately set up a landing page with a waitlist or payment link. Push your link to target communities and gather feedback from the first 10 users to iterate your product-market fit.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in the local machine graveyard. Leverage the power of AI-assisted engineering to turn your vision into a profitable reality. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
